name: Hobak Gonu
author: Traditional (Korea) / AGP port
description: Hobak Gonu (호박고누, 'pumpkin gonu'), a traditional Korean blockade game on an 11-point pumpkin-shaped board: a circle with an internal cross plus a three-point home row for each player. Three pieces each; a piece steps along a line to an empty point. Home rows are one-way funnels: endpoints feed the middle, the middle feeds the circle, and no piece may ever step back onto a home point (in the default ruleset the opponent's row is closed entirely; the 'trap' variant lets a piece enter the opponent's middle point and never leave). A player who cannot move loses; a repeated position is a draw. Fully solved: both rulesets are a draw under perfect play.
